### Training a Trading System on Labeled Data

A related approach trains a model to imitate a corpus of labeled trades directly,
mapping observed market states to actions without an intermediate forecasting
step. Formally, the model learns $f: X \rightarrow a$ by minimizing a loss over
labeled state-action pairs $(X_i, a_i^*)$:

$$\min_{\theta} \sum_{i=1}^{N} \ell(f_{\theta}(X_i), a_i^*)$$

The limitation is the same as in forecast-based approaches, expressed
differently: the optimization target is the labeled action, not the trading
objective. Since labels are typically constructed from historical rules or
expert behavior rather than from the actual utility function, minimizing
imitation error does not guarantee maximizing risk-adjusted return. The
labeling process itself introduces a prior about what constitutes optimal
behavior — a prior that may be wrong or regime-dependent.
